items.title: Musk says he’s building Terafab chip plant in Austin, Texas
items.description:
Grok, how do you make chips? | Image: Laura Normand / The Verge	

Elon Musk announced plans to build a Terafab plant in Austin, Texas, that will be jointly run by Tesla and SpaceX. The goal is to eventually build chips at scale for robotics, artificial intelligence, and space-based data centers for Musk's various companies.
Musk has expressed concern, as have other executives, about the chip industry's ability to keep up with demand as the AI industry has boomed. But building a chip fabrication plant is complex, requires billions of dollars, many years, and a ton of specialized equipment. And, as Bloomberg points out, Musk "has no background in semiconductor production and a history of over-promising on g …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Elon Musk announced plans to build a Terafab plant in Austin, Texas, that will be jointly run by Tesla and SpaceX. The goal is to eventually build chips at scale for robotics, artificial intelligence, and space-based data centers for Musk’s various companies.Musk has expressed concern, as have other executives, about the chip industry’s ability to keep up with demand as the AI industry has boomed. But building a chip fabrication plant is complex, requires billions of dollars, many years, and a ton of specialized equipment. And, as Bloomberg points out, Musk “has no background in semiconductor production and a history of over-promising on goals and timelines.”According to Bloomberg, Musk said, “We either build the Terafab or we don’t have the chips, and we need the chips, so we build the Terafab,” before outlining plans to produce chips that could support up to 200 gigawatts per year of computing power on Earth, and up to a terawatt in space. While Musk gave some details about the scale of the computing power he expected Terafab to produce, he offered no timeline for when it might meet those goals, or even when the facility might come online.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.titleZh: 马斯克称将在德州奥斯汀建设Terafab芯片工厂
items.descriptionZh:
埃隆·马斯克宣布计划在得克萨斯州奥斯汀建设一座名为Terafab的芯片制造工厂，该工厂将由特斯拉和SpaceX共同运营。其长期目标是为马斯克旗下多家公司的大规模机器人、人工智能及太空数据中心需求生产定制化芯片。

这一决策源于全球AI产业爆发式增长背景下，芯片供应链持续紧张的行业性焦虑。马斯克与其他科技领袖同样担忧现有芯片产能难以满足未来需求。然而，建设一座先进的芯片晶圆厂极其复杂，需要投入数百亿美元资金、耗时多年，并依赖大量尖端专用设备。彭博社同时指出，马斯克"并无半导体制造领域背景，且曾有过度承诺目标与时间表的历史记录"。

在公开表态中，马斯克强调了自建产能的必要性："我们要么建造Terafab，要么面临芯片短缺。既然我们需要芯片，就必须建造Terafab。"他进一步披露了该工厂的技术愿景——目标生产能支持年计算功率达200吉瓦（地面应用）乃至1太瓦（太空应用）的芯片。不过，尽管公布了计算规模的宏大目标，马斯克未提供工厂投产的具体时间表，也未说明实现该计算能力目标的预计期限。

该计划若得以推进，将显著增强马斯克旗下企业在自动驾驶、人形机器人、星链网络及太空计算基础设施等核心领域的供应链自主性与技术协同能力。但鉴于半导体制造业的高壁垒特性，这一跨越式布局的实际落地过程仍将面临技术积累、产业周期与资本投入的多重考验。
